Screen "Home" — reached by: Start page — open the application URL. Design: docs/design/Screenshot_from_2026-07-13_12-14-01.png 2. Screen "Screen 2" — reached by: click "learn more" link. Design: docs/design/Screenshot_from_2026-07-13_12-14-19.png Generate ONE Playwright flow that opens the start page, validates it against screen 1, then performs each navigation action and validates the resulting page against that screen's design. Deviations are defects. Do not pre-declare deviations before running. Task: Test the homepage against the attached design. --- Previous stage (Context Analyst) already ran; its summary/handoff: I'll start by reading the relevant files to understand the project structure and instructions. Avoid use in operations."` - Blue hyperlink: `"Learn more"` (left-aligned, below paragraph) - No other elements visible **Screen 2 — IANA page (reached by clicking "Learn more")** - White background body, light grey header/footer - IANA logo top-left + "Internet Assigned Numbers Authority" text - Top nav: `Domains`, `Protocols`, `Numbers`, `About` - `

` text: `"Example Domains"` - Body text includes RFC 2606 and RFC 6761 references (blue links) - `"Further Reading"` section heading - Bullet: `"IANA-managed Reserved Domains"` (blue link) - Footer: "Last revised 2017-05-13." - Footer columns: Domain Names, Number Resources, Protocols, About Us - Footer links: `"Privacy Policy"`, `"Terms of Service"` Now update `actualtestcases.csv` with the derived test cases and update `project-memory.md`: --- ## Context Analyst — Stage Complete ✅ Here is what was produced and what I observed from the design images. --- ### Design extraction (from attached images — authoritative) **Screen 1 — Home (`https://example.com/`)** | Element | Expected value (from design) | |---|---| | `

name: source-code-analysis
description: "Static, glass-box analysis of the application source (routes, components, handlers, API calls, business rules, selectors) that grounds test planning before any UI exploration. Use at the start of Test Planner Phase 2."
---

# Source Code Analysis

## Why
Source code is the primary source of truth (see `copilot-instructions.md` §3). UI exploration
*validates* what is found here — it never substitutes for it.

## Procedure

name: ui-exploration
description: "Black-box exploration of the running application UI to validate source-code analysis: screens, navigation graph, interactive elements. Use in Test Planner Phase 3, never by the Context Analyst (which is source-code-only)."
---

# UI Exploration (application under test)

## When to use
Only the **Test Planner** (and, transitively, `tests/ui-exploration.spec.ts` it may generate) explores
the running application. The Context Analyst and other agents must not n...read_file({"path":".github/skills/selector-extraction/SKILL.md"})---
name: selector-extraction
description: "Finding and recording stable selectors, in priority order, and building/maintaining specs/selectors.md. Use whenever a test needs a selector and when healing selector-category failures."
---

# Selector Extraction

## Priority order
1. `data-testid="..."`
2. `getByRole(...)` / `aria-label="..."`
3. stable, non-generated `id="..."`
4. `getByText(...)`
5. ⚠️ class names — last resort, expect churn
6. ❌ brittle XPath / deep CSS chains — never

page;\n  await activePage.waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded');\n  await activePage.screenshot({ path: 'projects/acme/specs/screenshots/screen2.png', fullPage: true });\n\n  const s2h1 = await activePage.locator('h1').first().textContent();\n  console.log('SCREEN2 h1:', s2h1);\n\n  const s2url = activePage.url();\n  console.log('SCREEN2 url:', s2url);\n\n  const newTab = newPage !== null;\n  console.log('SCREEN2 opened in new tab:', newTab);\n\n  // Nav items\n  const navItems = activePage.locator('nav a, header nav a, ul.nav a').filter({ hasText: /\\w/ });\n  const navTexts: string[] = [];\n  const ni = await navItems.count();\n  for (let i = 0; i < ni; i++) {\n    navTexts.push((await navItems.nth(i).textContent()) ?? '');\n  }\n  console.log('SCREEN2 nav items:', navTexts);\n\n  // Further reading\n  const fr = await activePage.locator('h2').allTextContents();\n  console.log('SCREEN2 h2 elements:', fr);\n\n  // Revision date\n  const bodyText = await activePage.locator('body').textContent();\n  const revised = bodyText?.includes('Last revised') ? 'YES' : 'NO';\n  console.log('SCREEN2 Last revised present:', revised);\n\n  // Privacy/ToS\n  const privacyCount = await activePage.locator('a:has-text(\"Privacy Policy\")').count();\n  const tosCount = await activePage.locator('a:has-text(\"Terms of Service\")').count();\n  console.log('SCREEN2 Privacy Policy links:', privacyCount, 'Terms of Service links:', tosCount);\n});\n"})BLOCKED (role boundary): the Test Planner stage may not write "projects/acme/tests/ui-exploration.spec.ts" — that file belongs to a different pipeline stage.
